steps.text: Peer review the sampling method.
Review and consult sampling method guidance documents and procedures to determine the level of validation associated with the method.
Address the precise procedures of the method when developing, using, or validating a sampling method.
Indicate whether the method uses a physical sampling or collection tool/technique, or whether the method is a field measurement process.
Specify conditions for either type of method.
Repeat some or all aspects of the validation study.
Clearly describe the components of the field sampling technique or field measurement processes subject to validation.
Define the matrix being sampled and the purpose of the sampling effort.

Clearly define all aspects of the equipment/instrument's set-up, calibration, and operation, as well as sample preparation, sample cleanup, and appropriate Quality Assurance/Quality Control (QA/QC) checks.
Clearly define the purpose of the sampling method and the intended use of the data.

Define and clearly describe the method scope and applicability, including validation components, analyte nature, matrix properties, range of analyte levels, advantages/limitations, equipment/supplies, and data quality objectives.

Evaluate the performance characteristics, including Selectivity, Method Uncertainty, Equipment/Instrument Calibration, Bias/Trueness, Detection and Quantitation Limits, Method Ruggedness, Sample Integrity, and Sample Size.
Evaluate the accuracy of the method from the performance characteristics.

Consider the uncertainties involved with the actual analytical measurement of the sample, including parameters such as analytical instrumental sensitivity, instrument calibration, analytical duplicate analysis, spike sample analysis, method bias, and matrix interferences.

Consider the uncertainties involved in the collection of the sample in the field, including parameters such as taking an adequate number of field samples, spatial homogeneity issues, adequate preservation of samples, and sampling equipment and techniques.
Plan how in-depth uncertainty is to be determined in advance before samples are collected in the field.
Provide an overall summary of the validation report in the Summary section.
Provide background information on sampling method development and state the purpose of the method in the Introduction.
Describe the sampling method validation technique/experimental design thoroughly, including a description of the test method/procedure and details of equipment/locations used with calibration status.
Discuss how the sampling method scope and applicability served to define the range of method performance.

Include a discussion of how selectivity was evaluated through the sampling of appropriate environmental media both with and without expected interferences, and a statement of how the sampling plan identified and addressed interferences.

Include a statement describing the decision of how in-depth uncertainty was determined and what planning took place in advance of samples being collected in the field, and a discussion of data quality objectives (DQOs) that were developed for the sampling method.

Include a statement focusing on the equipment that is used to measure or analyze samples in the field and how the required calibration, routine maintenance, and annual standardization are accomplished.

Include a discussion of how systematic errors were accounted for in the sampling plan through measurements of reasonably well known composition material, results comparisons to alternative methods or procedures, or using analyzed spikes.
Include a discussion of sampling method performance after minor changes are experienced in operating or environmental conditions.
Include a discussion of preserving the collected samples’ physical and chemical properties and security from the collection site to the analytical laboratory.

Include a discussion of how the sampling method determined the appropriate quantity of sample to be taken to support analysis, how the detection limit was defined and evaluated, how limits derived from mathematical definitions or statistical models were verified, and how detection limit as it applies to instrument detection capability was addressed.
Include a discussion of the number or items or the quantity constituting a sample, as appropriate for the method, whether the samples are composites or grab.
Perform validation planning as part of initial planning (e.g., directed planning process).
Integrate the contributions and requirements of all stakeholders into the validation planning.
Include the sampling validation plan as a section within the plan or as a stand-alone document attached as an appendix.
Address all required information in the sampling validation plan, including Purpose, Sampling Procedure, Field Variances, Data Validation, and Performance Characteristics.
Communicate the information and documentation identified in the validation plans to the laboratory as part of the Statement of Work (SOW).

preconditions.text: The sampling method is to be published.
Procedural changes are required after troubleshooting and optimizing a method.
Safety concerns and procedures must be addressed in both the sampling method and the validation plan.
Field measurement processes are to be performed.
Data quality objectives (DQOs) must be developed for the environmental site.

constraints.text: In emergency response situations, follow the emergency response method validation policy (Agency Policy Directive Number FEM-2010-01).
Address personal protective equipment and first aid.
Address physical hazards and chemicals that are toxic, corrosive, emit harmful or explosive vapors, or that are incompatible when mixed.

If an analytical test result is to be used for gaining only a general understanding of the local environmental conditions, then a precise representation of total uncertainty in the result may not be needed.
In situations involving regulatory action levels, public scrutiny of data results, or inspection audits, representation of uncertainty must be more detailed and precise.

The sampling validation plan must address: 1. Purpose of Validation; 2. Sampling Procedure (including test method principle, test procedures/conditions, equipment/facilities with calibration status, variables, samples to be taken, extraction/collection/retention efficiency, field and laboratory QC); 3. Field Variances; 4. Data Validation; 5. Performance Characteristics.
